This is a quick launch case study with Will Felkener so Will's business is he's a vet and he teaches his specialty is homeopathic medicine for animals and he's built up an email list of approximately 8,000 people so he took his existing product which was an ebook and It's an ebook and an audiobook bundle that normally sells for $45 and this is just one of the offers he has And what he did was he offered it at nearly a 50% off sale and He also added in a couple bonuses. I mean this is basically For the cost of well, I guess he did put together a special report And he's gonna do a one-hour Q&A webinar pretty easy to do to get on a webinar and answer questions for for an hour And he wrote six emails and he made almost $9,000 now another key thing here. It's been my experience over the years You know, there's two primary types of lists There's a list of prospects people that haven't bought from you yet yet and a list of clients or customers Who have bought from you? and in my experience someone who has bought from you and that is on your client list is worth 15 times more per dollar basis Than someone who hasn't bought from you in other words the next time you go to do a promotion for every subscriber That's on your client list and you compare that to a subscriber on your prospect list Your client list is going to generate 15 times greater results. So you might have a thousand person Prospect list and a thousand person Client list that prospect list might generate a thousand dollars in sales and that customer list will generate $15,000 in sales and that has been my experience over and over in 20 years in business and it varies on the offer and It can vary from business to business and I sometimes I've seen you know

I Wanted 50% of 50% of you likely missed the alerts So basically this is a pivot He just he just realized it wasn't a good day to close on Memorial Day So extended it one day and he gave his reason why you can totally do this you can totally do this and And this is the day if we scroll way back up This is the day where he did that extra hundred and forty one orders so this was his original closing day and Then this was the day he sent that email said duh I screwed up and so we got an additional hundred and forty one orders by doing it So again, you know, this is this reason why and that that's like a copywriting term When you when you have this what they call it reason why copy which is you you tell people why you're doing something and Like so the day before he said we're closing and then it came back and said, you know what I screwed up I shouldn't have closed on a holiday here in the US and so I decided to extend it one more day You can make the rules you can totally do this And then the final email is right here.
